title: Introduce Three.js renderer bootstrap
summary: Replace the Canvas 2D rendering shell with a Three.js WebGL renderer, perspective camera, scene graph, placeholder object, animation loop, and resize handler. The geometry systems (particleField, waveField) are adapted to the new Scene-based interface but their Canvas 2D draw logic is stubbed out for porting in future stories.

current_state.description: Implementation code is written in the working tree but has TypeScript errors and 88 failing tests. The tests were written against the old Canvas 2D API signatures and need updating to match the new Three.js-based interfaces.

steps.title: Install Three.js dependency
steps.verification: npm install succeeds, three resolves in imports

steps.title: Replace Canvas 2D scene bootstrap with Three.js WebGLRenderer
steps.description: Rewrite `initScene()` to create a WebGLRenderer (antialias, high-performance, capped pixel ratio), PerspectiveCamera (60° FOV, near 0.1, far 100, z=5), and Scene (black background). Attach renderer.domElement to the container. Return { renderer, scene, camera } instead of { canvas, ctx }.

issues.detail: `resolutionScale` parameter is declared but never read (TS6133). Either remove it or prefix with underscore.

steps.title: Create placeholder 3D object
steps.description: New file `placeholder.ts` adds a wireframe IcosahedronGeometry (radius 1.2, detail 1) with MeshStandardMaterial, ambient light (0.3 intensity), and directional light (0.7 intensity at [2,3,4]). Returns the mesh for rotation in the render loop.

steps.title: Update resize handler for Three.js
steps.description: Rewrite `attachResizeHandler()` to accept (WebGLRenderer, PerspectiveCamera) instead of (canvas, ctx, resolutionScale). On resize: update camera.aspect, call camera.updateProjectionMatrix(), and renderer.setSize().

steps.title: Update render loop for Three.js
steps.description: Change `startLoop()` signature from (canvas, ctx, deps) to (renderer, scene, camera, deps). Add `placeholderMesh` to LoopDeps. Replace ctx.fillRect clear with renderer.render(scene, camera). Add placeholder mesh rotation per frame. Pass Scene instead of ctx to geometry system init/draw.

steps.title: Update GeometrySystem interface to use Three.js Scene
steps.description: Change `GeometrySystem.init()` and `GeometrySystem.draw()` first parameter from `CanvasRenderingContext2D` to `Scene` (from three).

steps.title: Adapt geometry systems to new interface
steps.description: Update particleField and waveField to accept Scene instead of CanvasRenderingContext2D. Replace Canvas 2D draw implementations with TODO stubs (actual Three.js rendering deferred to future stories). Update modeManager to pass Scene through.

issues.detail: `enableSparkle` in particleField.ts declared but never read after Canvas 2D draw removal
`enableShimmer` in waveField.ts declared but never read after Canvas 2D draw removal

steps.title: Wire everything in main.ts
steps.description: Update main.ts to use new initScene() return type, add placeholder mesh, pass renderer/scene/camera to startLoop, comment out Canvas 2D mode manager and quality system imports (deferred to future stories).

steps.title: Fix TypeScript compilation errors
steps.description: Resolve 3 TS6133 errors: (1) remove or underscore-prefix unused `resolutionScale` in scene.ts, (2) remove or underscore-prefix unused `enableSparkle` in particleField.ts, (3) remove or underscore-prefix unused `enableShimmer` in waveField.ts.

steps.title: Update failing tests to match new Three.js interfaces
steps.description: 88 tests fail because they still reference Canvas 2D APIs. Tests need updating across multiple files to mock Three.js objects (WebGLRenderer, Scene, PerspectiveCamera, Mesh) instead of CanvasRenderingContext2D. Key test files to update: resize.test.ts (mock renderer/camera instead of canvas/ctx), scene.test.ts and scene-integration.test.ts (expect renderer/scene/camera return), renderLoop.test.ts (new startLoop signature), modeManager.test.ts (Scene instead of ctx), particleField.test.ts and waveField.test.ts (Scene-based init/draw), canvas-shell.test.ts (likely needs full rewrite or removal).

steps.title: Verify acceptance criteria
steps.description: Confirm: (1) WebGL renderer attached to #app container, (2) perspective camera renders wireframe icosahedron placeholder, (3) animation loop runs without console errors, (4) resize updates renderer size and camera aspect. Run `npm run build` to ensure production build passes.
verification: npm run test passes (all tests green)
No console errors on dev server load
Resize correctly updates camera aspect and renderer size

acceptance_criteria_mapping.WebGL renderer created and attached to a: step 2 (scene.ts: initScene creates WebGLRenderer, appends to container)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Perspective camera configured rendering: steps 2+3 (scene.ts: PerspectiveCamera 60° FOV; placeholder.ts: wireframe icosahedron)
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Resize updates renderer size and camera: step 4 (resize.ts: updates camera.aspect, projection matrix, and renderer.setSize)

risks.risk: Three.js mocking complexity in jsdom test environment
risks.mitigation: Create minimal Three.js mock objects (renderer with domElement, camera with aspect/updateProjectionMatrix, scene with add). WebGL context not available in jsdom — tests verify wiring, not actual rendering.

risks.risk: Canvas 2D geometry draw logic removed — visual regression for particle/wave systems
risks.mitigation: Intentional per ticket scope. Systems are stubbed with TODO comments. Canvas 2D draw code preserved in git history. Future stories (referenced in main.ts TODOs) will port to Three.js BufferGeometry/Points/Lines.

remaining_work_estimate.update_tests: bulk of remaining work — ~10 test files need Three.js mock objects and updated assertions
